mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment (UNRATE) and gains in payrolls (PAYEMS) signal strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns in instances where UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS > 100k are negative in > 50% of cases, or pairwise correlation between UNRATE/PAYEMS and SPX returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in periods with HOUST > 1.1M and RSAFS > 460B are negative in > 40% of cases, or the joint predictive power (R-squared) of HOUST/RSAFS drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 2.5% signals persistent inflation, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in periods with CPIAUCSL > 2.5% are positive in > 55% of cases, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL and SPX returns exceeds -0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production (INDPRO) index reflects expanding manufacturing activity, which increases corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in periods with INDPRO growth > 0.5% are negative in > 45% of cases, or the predictive power (IC) of INDPRO drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1) signals economic weakness that reduces corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in periods with GDPC1 < 0% are positive in > 50% of cases, or the correlation between GDPC1 and SPX returns exceeds -0.30.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low FEDFUNDS and high CPIAUCSL indicates a central bank's accommodative stance amid inflation, creating mixed valuation pressures that produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in periods with FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL > 2% are positive in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5% signals weakening labor market conditions that lower consumer spending confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE > 5% are positive in > 50% of cases, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns exceeds -0.20.
